name: Two-meat, Two Bean Chili
recipeIngredient: 5 pounds Ground round (15% lean ground beef)
2 pounds Hot or sweet Italian sausage, removed from casings -- crumb
2 Medium sweet red bell peppers, seeded and chopped
2 Medium green bell peppers, seeded and chopped
2 Fresh hot green chili peppers, seeded and minced
3 Cans (28-oz) whole tomatoes with tomato puree -- undrained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large soup kettle, cook the ground round and sausage over medium-high heat until seared (but not browned), about 10 minutes. Stir often.
2. Pour off excess fat. Add onions, red and green bell peppers, chili peppers, garlic, chili powder, salt, oregano, cumin, and bay leaves. Stir for 1 minute.
3. Add tomatoes with their puree, breaking up the tomatoes with a spoon. Bring to a simmer.
4. Reduce heat to low and cook until the liquid is reduced, about 1-1/2 hours.
5. In a small bowl, combine cornmeal and 1/2 cup water. Stir this mixture into the chili along with pink and black beans.
6. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the chili thickens, about 10 minutes.
7. (The chili can be made up to 3 days ahead. If desired, scrape off and discard solidified fat that rises to the surface. Reheat gently on top of the stove before serving.) Makes about 25 servings.
